title: Pharmacist
description: A pharmacist in India works in hospitals, clinics, or retail pharmacies, dispensing medicines, advising patients on medication usage, and monitoring patient health. They also assist in conducting medical research and quality control of pharmaceutical products. Pharmacists often work long hours, including night shifts, and may be required to work on weekends and holidays.
top_govt_colleges: National Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research, Hyderabad
Institute of Pharmacy, Nirma University, Ahmedabad
top_private_colleges: Manipal College of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Manipal
Sri Ramachandra College of Pharmacy, Chennai
Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani

career_progression.year_0_2: Assist in dispensing medicines, conduct basic medical research, and assist in quality control.
career_progression.year_3_5: Take on more responsibilities in research, quality control, and patient care. May also start to supervise junior pharmacists.
career_progression.year_6_10: Specialize in a particular area of pharmacy, such as clinical pharmacy or pharmaceutical research. May also take on leadership roles.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a retail pharmacy or a pharmaceutical manufacturing company.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities for working abroad in pharmaceutical companies or research institutions are limited, but possible with experience and specialized skills.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is low, and working hours can be long. However, job security and opportunities for advancement are good.
common_misconceptions: Pharmacists are not required to have a graduate degree
